brief_document.title: LibSqlStore struct and EventStore impl wiring
brief_document.purpose: Define the LibSqlStore type and wire up its EventStore trait implementation skeleton, with a constructor that opens a connection and ensures the schema. The method bodies are filled in by AS-004 (append), AS-005 (reads/query), and AS-006 (timers); this brief establishes the type, the constructor, and the trait impl block they extend.
brief_document.task: Create store.rs defining LibSqlStore (holding the libSQL connection from AS-002) as a Send + Sync + 'static type, with an open/connect constructor that builds it from a LibSqlConfig and ensures the schema. Add the EventStore impl block for LibSqlStore wiring in the methods that AS-004/005/006 implement. Confirm object-safety via Arc<dyn EventStore>. Method bodies may delegate to the append/read/timer modules those briefs create.

requirements.title: LibSqlStore type and constructor
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define LibSqlStore as a Send + Sync + 'static type holding the libSQL connection. WHEN constructed from a LibSqlConfig, THE SYSTEM SHALL open the connection (embedded or replica per the config) and ensure the schema exists before returning the store.
acceptance: store.rs defines struct LibSqlStore holding the connection/database handle from AS-002; the type is Send + Sync + 'static.
An async constructor (LibSqlStore::connect(config: LibSqlConfig) or equivalent) opens the connection and calls ensure_schema before returning.
A convenience LibSqlStore::open(path) constructs an Embedded config and delegates to the config constructor.
A test constructs a LibSqlStore at a tempfile path and asserts construction succeeds and the schema is present.
Connection/schema errors during construction surface as StoreError.

requirements.title: EventStore impl wiring and object-safety
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL implement the aion-store EventStore trait for LibSqlStore, with the impl block declaring every trait method, and the store SHALL be usable as Arc<dyn EventStore>.
acceptance: store.rs contains impl EventStore for LibSqlStore declaring append, read_history, list_active, query, schedule_timer, and expired_timers, each delegating to the module that owns its logic (append.rs, read.rs, timer.rs) — those bodies are completed in AS-004/005/006 and may be minimal-but-compiling stubs returning a clearly-marked unimplemented-path StoreError here only if a later brief in this same cluster replaces them (note this split in the task).
A compile/integration test constructs an Arc<dyn EventStore> from a LibSqlStore, proving the trait is object-safe as implemented.
The impl forwards the connection handle to the per-method modules so AS-004/005/006 implement against a shared store surface.

No method body in this brief silently swallows an error or returns Ok without doing its work; any not-yet-filled method returns a StoreError that the cluster's later briefs replace with the real implementation (the split is recorded so review knows AS-004/005/006 complete C14-C23).

requirements.title: Shared connection access for per-method modules
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL expose, within the crate, an accessor or shared handle by which append.rs, read.rs, and timer.rs reach the libSQL connection, so the per-method logic in later briefs operates against one store without re-opening connections.
acceptance: store.rs exposes a crate-internal way (e.g. a method returning &Connection or a cloned handle) for the per-method modules to obtain the connection.
Concurrency: the handle is shared safely (the store is Send + Sync); any interior lock is documented and lock poisoning is handled explicitly with no panicking unwrap.
A test confirms two sequential operations through the store reuse the same underlying database (e.g. a write then a read observe the same data) without opening a second connection.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement append's atomic body — that is AS-004 (this brief wires the method, AS-004 completes C14-C17).
SHALL NOT implement read_history/list_active/query bodies — that is AS-005 (completes C18-C21).
SHALL NOT implement schedule_timer/expired_timers bodies — that is AS-006 (completes C22-C23).
SHALL NOT add Serialize to config or introduce new Aion crate dependencies.
verification: A reviewer confirms LibSqlStore is Send + Sync + 'static and usable as Arc<dyn EventStore>, and that any interim method placeholders are tracked for completion by AS-004/005/006 (no swallowed errors, no permanent stub).
Mutex/RwLock poison handled explicitly where any interior lock exists.
No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new code.

checklist.text: LibSqlStore is a Send + Sync + 'static type holding the libSQL connection, constructed from a LibSqlConfig.
LibSqlStore implements the aion-store EventStore trait and is usable as Arc<dyn EventStore>.
An open/constructor associated function (e.g. LibSqlStore::open and/or LibSqlStore::connect) builds the store from a config and ensures the schema exists before returning.

stories.text: As an engine operator, I want a zero-infrastructure durable store I get by pointing at a file path, so that embedding Aion needs no database server to survive restarts.

constraints.text: unsafe_code = deny. No unsafe in the crate.
No #[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore] bypasses per CLAUDE.md. Tests needing a remote primary use a runtime env-gate, not #[ignore].
lib.rs is declarations and re-exports only; logic lives in named modules.
500-line file limit (excluding tests/comments/whitespace). The EventStore impl is split across append, read, and timer modules.
Depends only on aion-store (and transitively aion-core) among Aion crates — no dependency on the engine, beamr, or any other backend. Structural; must hold.
Raw SQL is permitted here because this crate IS a storage backend (the storage layer). No other crate issues SQL against this store; they go through the EventStore trait.
WorkflowStatus is derived from event history via aion-core's projection. No mutable stored status field; a cached projection must never diverge from the events.
LibSqlStore must pass the shared AC-007 conformance suite unmodified — the same behavioural standard as the oracle.
append's sequence guard and event inserts execute in a single libSQL transaction; a mismatch writes nothing and returns SequenceConflict — no partial batch is ever persisted.
LibSqlConfig is Deserialize-only and carries no hardcoded durability/WAL defaults; tunable values are surfaced for the operator to set, per CLAUDE.md no-assumed-defaults.
All libsql and serde errors are mapped into the StoreError taxonomy at the crate boundary; no foreign error type leaks across the EventStore surface.

resolved_context.intention: When an engine operator embeds Aion and writes no storage configuration, this is the store they get: a single file on disk, zero external infrastructure, full durability. The same crate serves the distributed path — the local file becomes an embedded replica syncing to a remote primary — without the operator learning a second backend. The in-memory reference store (cluster AC) proves the contract but forgets on restart; LibSqlStore makes durability the easy default, held to the identical behavioural standard as the oracle. A workflow that sleeps for months and resumes needs its history to survive every crash and reboot in between. When this cluster is done, an operator writes LibSqlStore::open("app.db") and has a production-grade durable event store the engine cannot tell apart from the oracle except that it remembers; the shared AC-007 conformance suite passes against it unchanged, plus a durability-specific suite proving state survives close-and-reopen.
